steps.title: Set up the linear pressure function
steps.content:
Pressure at any depth equals initial pressure plus the pressure increase from descent.

At sea level (depth = 0): 14.70 psi
For each foot descended: pressure increases by 0.44 psi

This gives us the linear function where $d$ is depth in feet:

steps.title: Substitute depth and calculate
steps.content:
Substitute $d = 105$ feet into the function:
$$P = 14.70 + 0.44(105)$$
Calculate the product: $0.44 \times 105 = 46.2$
$$P = 14.70 + 46.2 = 60.90 \text{ psi}$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
